item.title: Discrimination and Classification of Coke Variants via Hybrid E-tongue
item.description: Soft drinks contain different proportions of sugars, salts and other compounds, giving them a signature taste. We explore whether hybrid e-tongue (HET), utilized previously to discriminate pure tastes, can discern the tastes of Original (OC), Diet (DC) ...

item.title: Strategies Toward Pharmaceutical Self-reliance in Nepal for Strengthening the Health Care System.
item.description: Under the federal system of governance in Nepal, health facilities are supervised by the local and provincial governments. However, people have faced challenges in accessing quality, affordable medicines over the years. Lack of innovation and technology, ...

item.title: Comparing Symmetry-Adapted Perturbation Theory to Intermolecular Energy Decomposition Analysis Based on Absolutely Localized Molecular Orbitals
Synthordle: A web-based game for retrosynthetic training
item.description: Organic chemistry courses often suffer from high attrition and may benefit from instructional approaches beyond passive recall. Retrosynthetic analysis, a core skill for organic chemists, requires iterative practice and flexible reasoning rather than rote ...

item.title: Sustainable Ammonia Production from Biogas: A Review
item.description: Green hydrogen produced from biogas reforming offers a low-carbon feedstock route to sustainable ammonia synthesis, coupling waste valorization with decarbonized fertilizer and chemical production. This review examines the technical foundations of this ...

item.title: Modulation of Open-Shell Character in Dibenzoazaborine Dications through Amino-Substituent Modification
item.description: Oxidation of dibutylamino-substituted dibenzoazaborine Bu2NDBAB afforded the air-stable dication [Bu2NDBAB](SbF6)2 in 83% yield, with an NIR absorption band at 943 nm. Single-crystal X-ray diffraction revealed pronounced quinoidal distortion. Unlike the ...

item.title: A Curated 12,597-Structure MOF Library and Geometric Pre-Screening Protocol for CO2/N2/H2O Co-Adsorption
item.description: Pore size, pore shape, and framework density are among the most direct chemical handles available for tuning a metal–organic framework's (MOF's) affinity for CO2 over N2 and H2O, yet these structural descriptors are rarely used systematically to narrow a ...

item.title: Advancing polypharmacology-based drug discovery using deep generative models
Catalyst-Aware Regression for Oxidative Coupling of Methane: A Reproducible Small-Data Baseline
item.description: Oxidative coupling of methane (OCM) performance depends on both operating conditions and catalyst formulation. This study evaluates whether explicit catalyst identity improves prediction of C2 yield in a small experimental dataset containing 291 OCM runs. ...

item.title: A Chemist in the Loop: Deploying VLM Agents for Reaction Conditions Optimization Towards Manufacturing
item.description: The optimization of reaction conditions to max-imize product yield is a critical bottleneck in chemical innovation, where every additional iteration, in the lab or the manufacturing plant, adds to costs of reagents, time, and expert effort. We report the ...

item.title: Pouch Versus Press Cells – How Cell Configuration Dictates Low-Pressure Performance in Sulfide Solid-State Batteries
item.description: Sulfide-based solid-state batteries (SSBs) offer the potential for higher energy densities compared to state-of-the-art lithium-ion batteries. Although impressive electrochemical performance has already been demonstrated, these results are predominantly ...

item.title: Trace-State Validation for Agentic Computational Chemistry Workflows
item.description: Agentic chemistry workflows coordinate simulation tools, intermediate records, and automated decisions across agents or agent-like stages. They can fail before any numerical result is produced: a downstream agent or workflow step may act on exchanged ...

item.title: Deciphering the Mechanism and Participation of CF2 carbene as a surrogate in Carbonylative Palladium-Catalyzed Multicomponent Reactions: The Origin of Ligand-
item.description: Difluorocarbene precursors have recently garnered significant attention as versatile C1 synthons for a range of transformations. Among these, carbonylative processes are proposed to proceed via in situ generation of free CO from the difluorocarbene unit, ...

item.title: Engineering Cooperative Multi-Domain DNA Probes for Programmable Nucleic Acid Detection
item.description: Nucleic acids are powerful recognition elements because base pairing enables straightforward probe design, yet selective target recognition under physiological conditions remains challenging when specificity cannot rely on melting-based discrimination. ...

item.title: FFEMR: Foundation Feature-enhanced Molecular Representation for High-Throughput Molecular Optimization
item.description: There is a growing demand for tailormade molecules addressing specific applications, such as green solvents, pharmaceutical ingredients and other functional materials. High-quality chemical encodings are essential for effective molecular optimization to ...

item.title: Soft-templated carbon models elucidate the anomalous increase of capacitance in nanopores
item.description: Supercapacitors are energy storage devices characterized by fast charging capabilities. The discovery of enhanced capacitance in nanoporous carbons radically challenged the understanding of their charging mechanism, sparkling the development of in situ ...

item.title: physcode: A PySCF-Based Gaussian-Style Python Interface for Teaching Quantum Chemistry Beyond the Black Box
item.description: Commercial quantum chemistry packages operate as opaque black boxes for most students. We present physcode, an open-source Python pedagogical interface built around PySCF that reads standard Gaussian-format .gjf input and writes Gaussian-compatible .log ...

item.title: A Compositional Operator Framework for Long-Range Particle Dynamics in Modular Microfluidic Networks
item.description: This work proposes a compositional operator framework in which local exit operators represent particle transport within individual microfluidic modules. The operator maps an encoded representation of the inlet interface state and module context to an exit ...

item.title: Concentration-dependent Transition in the Rate-determining Step of Hydrogen Evolution Driven by Pyridinium-tethered Mononuclear and Dinuclear Platinum Photocatalysts
item.description: Our previous studies on the hydrogen evolution reaction (HER) catalyzed by Pt(II)-based molecular catalysts often demonstrated a second-order dependence of the HER rate on the catalyst concentration, suggesting the importance of stabilizing a ...

item.title: Model of the Henrys: Eyring, Frank, and the Foundations for Predicting Transport Coefficients
item.description: Henry Eyring’s absolute rate theory provides a microscopic picture of liquid transport based on molecular jumps, while Henry Frank’s free volume theory relates entropy changes to an effective cage volume in condensed phases. In this work, we integrate ...

item.title: Peroxide-Mediated Pseudo-Current Multiplication: A Predictive Strategy to Enhance Anodic Current in Photoelectrochemical Hydrogen Production
item.description: Photoelectrochemical (PEC) water splitting converts solar energy into hydrogen by coupling anodic water oxidation with cathodic hydrogen evolution. In this framework, the photoanode is not primarily valuable because it produces oxygen; rather, it is ...

item.title: Machine Learning for Predicting the Configuration of Small Molecules
item.description: The configuration of small molecules plays a key role in their measurable properties. This includes their spatial arrangement in 3-dimension space, giving rise to stereoisomers. As different stereoisomers can have vastly different biological effects, its ...

item.title: TopoTunnelMiner : Topology-Guided Mining and Comparative Analysis of Dynamic Protein Tunnels
item.description: The analysis of protein tunnels is essential for elucidating the dynamic variations of substrate entry, product release, and molecular transport pathways. In molecular dynamics simulations, however, continuous conformational motion shifts tunnel ...

item.title: Optimization of pyrazolopyridine derivatives for the treatment of human African trypanosomiasis
item.description: Despite recent advances in the development of therapeutics for human African trypanosomiasis, new treatments are needed to populate the drug pipeline in case of failure and resistance. A previously reported high throughput screen of human kinase ...

item.title: Reversible Acidification Enables Closed-Loop Total Separation of Olivine
item.description: Silicate minerals such as olivine are abundant in mineral deposits and mine tailings, and contain alkalinity, Mg2+, Fe2+, and other trace metals. While historically considered waste, the total processing and separation of these components can generate MgO ...

item.title: Shaping the electric field: Overcoming geometric inhomogeneities in the electrochemical synthesis of mesoporous materials
item.description: (Dated: July 17, 2026) Integrating porous systems such as mesoporous silicon (pSi) into reliable macroscopic devices for sensing, photonics and energy storage requires stringent control over their structural homogeneity. While radial variations in layer ...

item.title: Dual PCET-Enabled Chemoselective Decarboxylative Sulfinylation with N-Sulfinyl Phthalimides as Bench-Stable Sulfinyl Radical Precursors
item.description: Despite their synthetic versatility, sulfinyl radicals have seen limited practical use over six decades, mainly due to the shortage of general and accessible precursors for their controlled generation. Herein, we disclose N-sulfinyl phthalimides as a new ...

item.title: A Generalized Framework Based on Enveloping Distribution Sampling for Calculating Geometrical Free-Energy Differences
item.description: Enveloping distribution sampling (EDS) is an efficient multistate free-energy method that has been mainly applied to estimate alchemical transformations, e.g., for relative solvation or binding free energies. In such simulations, each end-state in the ...

item.description: DNA-encoded libraries (DELs) allow screening of extremely large chemical spaces by tagging compounds with DNA barcodes. As these datasets grow in scale and complexity, machine learning has become essential for signal denoising and binder identification. ...

item.title: FolioClip: Comprehensive Plant Health Monitoring and Early Stress Detection with Real-Time Multimodal Wearable Sensing and Online Machine Learning
item.description: Wearable plant sensing systems for simultaneous biochemical and physiological monitoring with real-time multimodal data analysis remain limited. Here, we present FolioClip, a multimodal wearable patch that continuously monitors leaf temperature, humidity, ...

item.description: ChemGNN, a deep-learning-accelerated framework that learns a graph-based surrogate of membrane transport and actively proposes high-performance designs. Each CNT configuration is encoded as a heterogeneous graph in which tube nodes carry geometry and ...

item.description: In this study, a simple, rapid, and reliable methodology was developed for measuring the injected sample volume in capillary electrophoresis and related techniques. The method does not require a calibration curve, nor knowledge of sample viscosity, ...

item.title: Ultrafast Capture of Heavy Metals from Water by the Anionic MOF SU-102: Synergistic Experimental and DFT Insights
item.description: Heavy-metal contamination of water remains a major environmental challenge, requiring adsorbents capable of efficient removal under realistic operating conditions while maintaining rapid kinetics, selectivity, and reusability. Although metal–organic ...

item.title: Fe/P Incorporation Sequence Shapes the Evolution of Fe–Nx Sites into FePx Nanoparticles in ZIF-8-Derived Fe-P-N-C Catalysts for Oxygen Reduction in Near-Neutral Media
item.description: Despite extensive research, platinum-group-metal-free electrocatalysts for oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) remain under active development due to their potential largescale application in electrochemical energy conversion and storage devices. Among them, ...

item.title: AnhDis: A Scheme for Anharmonic Nuclear Ensembles and Absorption Spectra
item.description: The Nuclear Ensemble Approach (NEA), typically implemented using the harmonic Wigner approximation, has shown significant limitations when applied to molecules with pronounced anharmonicity. The harmonic model frequently samples unphysical regions of the ...

item.title: An Agentic AI Framework for Hepatotoxicity Prediction: Integrating Multi-Descriptor Deep Learning Ensembles with Reasoning-Based Escalation
item.description: Drug-induced liver injury (DILI) remains one of the most common causes of late-stage clinical trial failures and post-market drug withdrawals, creating an urgent need for reliable, mechanistically grounded, and computationally efficient early-stage ...

item.title: Nanobubbles Are Real — Now What? Perspective on Nanobubble Generation and Measurement, Knowledge Gaps, and Priorities for Future Research
item.description: Nanobubbles (NBs), gas-filled bubbles with diameters below 1 µm, have attracted significant interest due to their unusual stability in water and their potential to enhance processes across environmental, agricultural, medical, energy, and food ...

item.title: Native Top-Down Mass Spectrometry Links Structure and Gas-Phase Stability of ERα-LBD
Reinventing the Mitsunobu Reaction with Iminophosphorane Reagents
item.description: The Mitsunobu reaction is one of the most celebrated reactions in organic synthesis, enabling stereospecific C–O, C–N, and C–S bond formation from abundant alcohol feedstocks. However, its widespread use is constrained by explosive reagents, laborious ...

item.title: Pauli Charges Improve the Training of Machine-Learned Interatomic Potentials
item.description: Despite the utility of machine-learned interatomic potentials (MLIPs), they can be difficult to train and use due to a lack of well-defined methods to assess their accuracy. This problem can be mitigated by combining traditional MLIPs with pair potentials ...

item.title: Conformational Flexibility and Size of Large trans-Vinylene-Bridged Thiaporphyrinoids Influences Boron Coordination Modes
item.description: Two π-extended core-modified porphyrinoids were synthetized and structurally characterized to probe boron-induced geometry switching and modulation of electron delocalization.Trans-vinylene-bridged hexathia-dodecaphyrin forms the largest, fully ...

item.title: Bright Red Solid-State Circularly Polarized Luminescence from Crystalline H-Aggregated Perylene Bisimide
item.description: Achieving deep-red circularly polarized luminescence (CPL) in the solid state from purely organic molecules remains challenging because molecular transitions are magnetic dipole-forbidden and nonradiative decay is enhanced by the energy-gap law. We ...

item.title: Which Metrics Best Capture Protein Structural Change in Molecular Dynamics Simulations? Evaluating Score Combinations and Force Field Effects
item.description: The scores RMSD, TM-score, GDT, lDDT, SphereGrinder, CAD, QCS, FlexE, and MolProbity provide an automated, comprehensive assessment of conformational changes and structural quality of proteins and thus represent a promising tool for routine use in ...

item.title: Predictive Modelling Enables Exploration of the Anticancer Potential of Ruthenium(II) Polypyridyl Complexes

item.description: Heterometallic complexes containing multiple heavier tetrylidyne ligands (RE; E = Si, Ge, Sn, Pb) are promising for cooperative bond activation but are rare because RE units tend to oligomerize. We present the synthesis, electronic structure, and ...

item.title: Explainable Machine Learning for Low-Emission Methane Tri-Reforming: Carbon Formation, Hydrogen Production, and Operating-Window Optimization
item.description: Tri-reforming of methane (TRM) is a promising route for producing hydrogen-rich syngas while partially utilizing CO2, but its deployment is limited by trade-offs among methane conversion, syngas composition, reactor duty, and carbon deposition. In this ...

item.description: Molten chloride salt is a widely studied high-temperature electrolyte, with established use in pyrochemical reprocessing and electrorefining, and frequent use as a model system for concentrated solar power and molten salt reactor applications. Chromium (...

item.title: Development of ionically crosslinked network in metal oxide based carboxylated acrylonitrile butadiene copolymer with self-healing characteristics
item.description: Ionic clusters produced during the crosslinking of elastomers with zinc oxide influence the overall mechanical performance of ionic elastomers such as carboxylated nitrile rubber (XNBR). The composition of these ionic aggregates is investigated for the ...

item.title: A Foundation Model for Accurate Atomistic Simulations in Drug Design
item.description: While artificial intelligence has revolutionized the prediction of static protein structures, characterizing their dynamics and interactions with drug candidates remains a computational bottleneck. Here, we introduce FeNNix-Bio1, a foundation machine ...

item.title: Sources of the Most Prevalent Persistent Organic Pollutant in the Global Atmosphere
item.description: Global atmospheric contamination with hexachlorobuta-1,3-diene (HCBD) has been rising rapidly for the past decade, despite its listing as a persistent organic pollutant (POP) under the Stockholm Convention. The unintentional nature of its production makes ...

item.title: From Molecular Intent to Auditable Computation: Black-Box Evaluation of a Text-to-Experiment Platform
item.description: Natural-language interfaces can simplify computational chemistry workflows, but a plausible model response does not establish that an experiment is executable, traceable, or scientifically correct. We evaluate a proprietary text-to-experiment platform ...

item.title: Foldamers with Two Distinct Helical Motifs Controlled by Solvent
item.description: Abiotic foldamers that switch between distinct, well-defined geometries are rare. Here, we report a structurally simple series of oligomers with alternatingortho-phenylene and 2,3-pyrazinylene repeat units. Deconvolution of variable-temperature ¹H NMR ...

item.title: The Quantum-Volume and Bragg-Williams Equations of State
item.description: The quantum-volume (qv) and Bragg-Williams equations of state, as derived from a lattice gas model, are exploited. The qv equation of state includes a temperaturedependent excluded volume related to the thermal wavelength included in the translational ...

item.title: Cooperative Heterogeneous–Homogeneous Catalysis Enables Aprotic H2O2 Synthesis and Tandem Organic Oxidations
item.description: We show that cooperation of heterogeneous metal and homogeneous Brønsted-acid catalysts enables direct synthesis of hydrogen peroxide (H2+ O2→ H2O2) in aprotic solvents. By studying different molecular acids, we discover that the selectivity of H2O2vs...

item.title: Distribution of Relaxation Times through the Lens of Distribution Theory
item.description: The distribution of relaxation times (DRT) model is widely used to deconvolve electrochemical impedance spectra into timescale distributions. However, standard DRT formulations assume integrable impedance. This condition is violated by electrochemical ...

item.title: From Photon Spiral Motion to Superheavy Elements: A Unified Analytical Atomic-Molecular Energy Theory Based on Quantum Phase Vortices and Relativistic Corrections
item.description: Within the constructive theoretical framework, this work establishes the Ether-Vortex Atomic and Molecular Model (EVAM), a fully analytical energy scheme covering the entire periodic table with atomic numbers from to . Three foundational constructive ...

item.description: Absorption, distribution, metabolism, excretion and toxicity (ADMET) measurements are often reported only as upper or lower bounds because an assay does not resolve activity beyond its tested concentration range. These inequality­labelled observations ...

item.title: Photocatalytic Pavement as an Ecosystem Technology: A Retrofit Pathway to Negative-Carbon Road Systems
item.description: Photocatalytic pavement — road and airfield surfaces incorporating titanium dioxide (TiO2) — targets the diffuse, use-phase, mobile-source emissions that constitute the majority of transportation’s greenhouse-gas footprint. We report independent ...

item.title: A curated list of material properties for 30 photovoltaic absorbers
item.description: I present a highly commented list of eight material properties for 28 experimentally synthesized and 10 computationally modeled photovoltaic absorbers (30 distinct materials). These properties are used to evaluate the recently proposed ΓPVfigure of merit ...

item.title: Modeling Glass-Transition Temperatures with an Emphasis on Pharmaceutical Compounds
item.description: The glass-transition temperature (Tg) marks the transition of an amorphous material from a rigid glassy state to a more mobile rubbery or supercooled-liquid state. Tg indicates molecular mobility, crystallization tendency, and physical stability in ...

item.title: Grammar for Chemically Auditable MOF Adsorption Machine Learning
item.description: Machine learning is now routinely used to screen metal–organic frameworks (MOFs), but the object seen by a model is not the crystal itself. Instead, it is a representation: a selected set of geometric, topological, chemical, or learned descriptors that ...

item.description: Accurate prediction of remaining useful life (RUL) for lithium-ion batteries is critical for ensuring reliability and safety in energy storage systems. This paper introduces a novel hybrid deep learning architecture that synergistically combines ...

item.description: In this work we perform simultaneous phase and chemical equilibrium calculation using the Group Contribution with Association equation of state (GCA-EOS), aiming to assess reactive systems in the framework of biorefinery development. The lack of ...

item.description: Valorization of aquaculture waste into biofuels and biomaterials is crucial to advancing a low-carbon bioeconomy. However, the inherent variability in the fatty acid composition introduces uncertainties in conversion efficiencies during ...

item.title: On Contemporary Tafel Analysis
item.description: Tafel slope analysis has been the cornerstone of mechanistic interpretation in multi-step electrochemical reactions for nearly a century, underpinning our understanding of reactions from hydrogen evolution to carbon dioxide reduction. Here we demonstrate ...

item.title: Cross-species principles of silk protein assembly from metastable feedstocks to hierarchical materials
item.description: Silk is one of nature’s most versatile structural biomaterials, produced independently by a vast diversity of species across insects, spiders, marine invertebrates, crustaceans, and even select vertebrates. Despite their evolutionary distance and ...

item.title: Symmetry Numbers: A Flexible Approach for Molecules and Transition States
item.description: Symmetry numbers are essential corrections in thermochemistry and chemical kinetics, but their reliable assignment remains difficult in automated workflows, especially for stereochemical, fluxional, cyclic, and transition-state structures. We present a ...

item.title: Structural flexibility of styrylpyrene chromophore in reproducing solvent-induced spectral shifts
item.description: Solvatochromic shifts in UV-vis absorption of photoreactive chromophores and microenvironment effects in their light-induced [2+2] cycloaddition reactions are presently not fully understood. Spectral shifts observed experimentally could not be yet ...

item.title: Sensitive Derivatization-Free HPLC-MS Method For Quantification of Cyclohexylamine as a Process-Related Impurity in Rosuvastatin calcium salt
item.description: Cyclohexylamine is an alicyclic amine that may be introduced as a process-related impurity during the manufacture of rosuvastatin calcium. Due to its potential toxicological relevance, sensitive and selective analytical methods are required to monitor ...

item.description: Liquid-phase adsorption (LPA) measurements are less frequently applied for the characterisation of porous materials than gas adsorption, although, for a variety of applications, such as (water) purification or heterogeneous catalysis, the liquid phase ...

item.title: Quantum defect density controls carbon nanotube sensor sensitivity via photophysical cooperativity
item.description: Single-walled carbon nanotubes (SWCNTs) fluoresce in the near-infrared (NIR), which overlaps with the biological transparency window. Therefore, SWCNTs are used as versatile materials for (bio)photonics. Controlled covalent functionalization of SWCNTs ...
